# oBerry

> Modern, reactive, jQuery-like DOM library. Lightweight (3.5kB gzipped), TypeScript-first, no build setup required. Provides fine-grained signal-based reactivity, a chainable DOM API, and Web Component-based reusable components.

## Installation

```sh
npm create oberry       # scaffold a new project
npm install oberry      # add to existing project
```

## Core Concepts

- **`$()`** — Primary selector/wrapper. Accepts CSS selectors, DOM elements, NodeLists, or arrays. Returns a chainable `ElementWrapper`.
- **`$ref()`** — Creates a reactive signal. Read by calling it (`count()`), write by calling with a value (`count(5)`) or updater (`count(prev => prev + 1)`).
- **`$computed()`** — Derives a read-only reactive value from other refs/computeds. Auto-tracks dependencies.
- **`$effect()`** — Runs a side-effect callback immediately and re-runs when its reactive dependencies change. Returns a stop function.
- **`$effectScope()`** — Groups multiple effects so they can all be stopped at once. Returns a stop function.
- **`$component()`** — Registers a custom HTML element (Web Component) with scoped DOM, reactive props, Shadow DOM, and lifecycle hooks.
- **`$new()`** — Creates new DOM elements with optional children, returning an `ElementWrapper`.
- **`Plugin` / `use()`** — Extend `ElementWrapper` with custom chainable methods.

## Reactivity API

### $ref

```ts
import { $ref } from "oberry";
const count = $ref(0);
count();          // read → 0
count(5);         // write
count(p => p+1);  // updater
```

### $computed

```ts
import { $computed } from "oberry";
const total = $computed(() => price() * qty());
total(); // read-only
```

### $effect

```ts
import { $effect } from "oberry";
const stop = $effect(() => {
  document.title = `Count: ${count()}`;
});
stop(); // dispose
```

### $effectScope

```ts
import { $effectScope } from "oberry";
const stop = $effectScope(() => {
  $effect(() => { /* ... */ });
  $effect(() => { /* ... */ });
});
stop(); // stops all effects in scope
```

## DOM Selection & ElementWrapper

```ts
$('.btn')                  // CSS selector
$(document.getElementById('app'))  // native element
$(nodeList)                // NodeList / HTMLCollection
$([el1, el2])              // array of elements
```

### Text & HTML

- `.text()` — get innerText of first element
- `.text(value)` — set innerText of all elements
- `.html()` — get innerHTML of first element
- `.html(value)` — set innerHTML of all elements

### Attributes

- `.attr(key)` — get attribute value
- `.attr(key, value)` — set attribute
- `.data(key)` — get data attribute
- `.data(key, value)` — set data attribute
- `.id()` — get id
- `.id(value)` — set id (first element only)
- `.value()` — get input value
- `.value(v)` — set input value

### Classes

- `.class.add('name')` — add class(es), space-separated
- `.class.remove('name')` — remove class
- `.class.toggle('name')` — toggle class
- `.class.has('name')` — check if first element has class
- `.class.get()` — returns `string[]` of classes on first element

### Styles & Dimensions

- `.css({ prop: value })` — set inline styles
- `.width()` / `.width(n)` — get/set width
- `.height()` / `.height(n)` — get/set height
- `.offset()` — `{ top, left }` relative to document
- `.position()` — `{ top, left }` relative to offset parent

### Visibility

- `.show()` — restore display
- `.hide()` — set display: none (saves original value)
- `.toggle()` — toggle visibility

### DOM Manipulation

- `.append(content)` — insert at end of element
- `.prepend(content)` — insert at beginning of element
- `.before(content)` — insert before element (sibling)
- `.after(content)` — insert after element (sibling)
- `.remove()` — remove elements from DOM
- `.empty()` — clear innerHTML
- `.clone(deep?)` — clone elements (deep by default)

### Reactive Bindings

- `.bind(ref)` — bind ref to innerText
- `.bindHTML(ref)` — bind ref to innerHTML
- `.bindInput(ref)` — two-way bind ref to input value
- `.bindAttr(attr, ref)` — bind ref to an attribute

### Events

- `.on(event, callback)` — add event listener
- `.off(event, callback)` — remove event listener
- `.once(event, callback)` — one-time event listener

### Navigation / Traversal

- `.children(selector?)` — direct children
- `.find(selector)` — all matching descendants
- `.parent()` — parent of first element
- `.closest(selector)` — nearest matching ancestor
- `.siblings(selector?)` — all siblings

### Filtering

- `.filter(selector | predicate)` — keep matching elements
- `.not(selector)` — exclude matching elements
- `.eq(index)` — element at index
- `.first()` — first element
- `.last()` — last element
- `.odd()` — 1-based odd-indexed elements
- `.even()` — 1-based even-indexed elements

### Iteration

- `.forEach(cb)` — iterate; each el passed as `ElementWrapper`
- `.map(cb)` — transform to plain array
- `.reduce(cb, initial)` — accumulate a value

### Inspection

- `.length()` — number of elements
- `.isEmpty()` — true if no elements
- `.is(selector)` — true if first element matches selector
- `.getArray()` — returns raw `HTMLElement[]`

## Components ($component)

```ts
import { $ref, $component } from "oberry";

$component<'start'>(
  'x-counter',
  ({ $, props, onMounted, onUnmounted, $host, $emit }) => {
    const { start } = props; // Ref<string | undefined>
    const count = $ref(Number(start() ?? 0));

    onMounted(() => {
      $('#counter').bind(count);
      $('button').on('click', () => count(p => p + 1));
    });

    onUnmounted(() => { /* cleanup */ });

    return `
      <h1 id="counter">${start() ?? 0}</h1>
      <button>+</button>
    `;
  }
);
```

```html
<x-counter start="10"></x-counter>
```

### ComponentContext properties

| Property | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `$` | `(selector) => ElementWrapper` | Scoped selector (Shadow DOM only) |
| `$host` | `ElementWrapper` | Wraps the host custom element |
| `props` | `Record<string, Ref<string \| undefined>>` | Reactive attribute refs |
| `onMounted` | `(cb) => void` | Callback after render |
| `onUnmounted` | `(cb) => void` | Cleanup before destroy |
| `$emit` | `(event, detail?) => void` | Dispatch CustomEvent (bubbles, composed) |

## Creating Elements ($new)

```ts
import { $new } from "oberry";

const list = $new(
  'ul',
  $new('li').text('Item #1'),
  $new('li').text('Item #2'),
);
$('body').append(list);
```

## Plugin System

```ts
import { Plugin, use } from "oberry";

const myPlugin = new Plugin('myPlugin', extend => {
  extend('highlight', function (color = 'yellow') {
    return this.css({ backgroundColor: color });
  });
});

use(myPlugin); // installing the same plugin twice is a no-op

$('.el').highlight('orange');
```

> Use `function` (not arrow functions) inside `extend()` so `this` refers to the `ElementWrapper` instance.

## TypeScript

- `Ref<T>` — type for `$ref` values
- `$<HTMLButtonElement>('.btn')` — generic for typed element wrappers
- `$component<'prop1' | 'prop2'>('tag', fn)` — generic for typed props autocomplete

## Links

- Docs: https://oberry.pages.dev/docs/introduction
- Quick Start: https://oberry.pages.dev/docs/quick-start
- GitHub: https://github.com/oberryjs/oBerry
